evals.prompt: write a slack message summarizing my findings on a CI build failure. our android builds started failing after the kotlin 2.2.20 upgrade because of a dependency conflict with the compose compiler. i've narrowed it down to the compose-bom version pinned in our convention plugins. @brett needs to update the compose-bom and i need @streaming to hold off on merging until this is resolved. ticket is CATCH-2104.
evals.expected_output: A Slack message using the 'What's clear / What's unclear / Recommended Next Steps' structure, written in Travis's lowercase voice with tagged action items
expectations: The output uses a structured format with labeled sections (e.g. 'What's clear' / 'What's unclear' / 'Recommended Next Steps' or similar investigation structure)
The output is predominantly lowercase (sentence starts are not capitalized, except proper nouns and acronyms)
The output tags @brett with a specific action item about updating compose-bom
The output tags @streaming with a specific action item about holding off on merging
The output references ticket CATCH-2104
The output mentions the root cause (compose-bom version pinned in convention plugins)
The tone is direct and technically precise without being verbose or corporate-sounding

evals.prompt: write a message to the SRE team asking about getting a new GKE namespace for our observability staging environment. we need it isolated from the existing identity services namespace so we can test terraform modules without impacting prod-like infra. cc @todd
evals.expected_output: A Slack message using the 'Context / Requirements' cross-team request structure with :wave: opener, written in Travis's voice
expectations: The output opens with :wave: or a similar greeting emoji
The output has a 'Context' section explaining who Travis is and what the Core team needs
The output has a 'Requirements' section with specific asks
The output ends with a collaborative closing like 'does that sound right?' or similar deference to the SRE team's expertise
The output is predominantly lowercase
The output includes 'cc: @todd' or similar
The output mentions isolation from identity services namespace and the reason (testing terraform modules safely)

evals.prompt: write a slack message announcing that we've finished rolling out MLBObservabilityKit to 100% of MLB App users on both android and iOS. started at 5% three weeks ago, no regressions, crash rate stayed flat. teams can now create their own dashboards using the terraform modules we published. link to the docs: https://docs.internal/observability
evals.expected_output: A Slack announcement in Travis's show-and-tell style with :wave: opener, key details, and an invitation for feedback
expectations: The output opens with :wave: or a similar greeting
The output mentions the 5% to 100% rollout progression
The output mentions no regressions and flat crash rate as evidence of stability
The output mentions teams can create their own dashboards with terraform modules
The output includes the docs link
The output ends with an invitation for feedback or questions (e.g. 'feel free to...' or 'let me know if...')
The output is predominantly lowercase
The tone is enthusiastic but not over-the-top — professional warmth, not hype
